title: ABV IIITM Recruitment 2026 - Apply Offline for Assistant Engineer & Junior Engineer Posts
organization: Atal Bihari Vajpayee-Indian Institute of Information Technology & Management, Gwalior
short_info: The Atal Bihari Vajpayee Indian Institute of Information Technology and Management Gwalior (ABV IIITM) has officially announced the Non-Faculty Positions Recruitment 2026 with 2 openings. The detailed notification was published on 04 May 2026 at the official website. Candidates who meet the age criteria of up to 35 years (Assistant Engineer) and 32 years (Junior Engineer) and hold qualifications such as Diploma/Degree in Engineering with relevant experience can now submit their offline applications before 01 June 2026. Direct links to download the notification and apply are shared below.

age_limit.age_details: Assistant Engineer: Max 35 years (OBC), Junior Engineer: Max 32 years (SC)
age_limit.details: Assistant Engineer (Electrical): Maximum age limit is 35 years. Age relaxation for OBC candidates as per Govt. of India norms. Junior Engineer (Civil): Maximum age limit is 32 years. Age relaxation for SC candidates as per Govt. of India norms.

qualification.education_qualification: Diploma/Degree in Engineering with relevant experience.
qualification.details: Assistant Engineer (Electrical): 1st Class Diploma in Electrical Engineering or equivalent Degree as per the nomenclature recognized by AICTE as Diploma in Electrical Engineering, along with 08 Years of post qualification Experience. 1st Class B.E./B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering or equivalent Degree as per the nomenclature recognized by AICTE in Electrical Engineering Stream with 06 Years of post qualification Experience. Experience must be in regular scale obtained from institutions like IITs/IISc/IIMs/IIITs/NITs IISERs or similar such Centrally/State funded institutions/Government Organizations/Public Sectors Units. Work experience obtained during the qualifying academic programme/s must not be considered. Junior Engineer (Civil): 1st Class Diploma in Civil Engineering or equivalent Diploma as per the nomenclature recognized by AICTE as Diploma in Civil Engineering Stream with 05 Years of post qualification Experience. 1st Class B.E./B.Tech. in Civil Engineering or equivalent Degree as per the nomenclature recognized by AICTE in Civil Engineering Stream with 02 Years of post qualification Experience. Experience must be in regular scale obtained from institutions like IITs/IISc/IIMs/IIITs/NITs /IISERs or similar such Centrally funded institutions/Government Organizations/ Public Sectors Units. Work experience obtained during the qualifying academic programme/s must not be considered.

salary_details.pay_scale: Pay Level 7 and Level 6 as per 7th CPC

selection_process: Written Test Interview Trade Test Game Test
how_to_apply: Candidates who wish to apply for ABV IIITM Various Posts Recruitment 2026 through offline mode should follow the steps below.
Read the official notification carefully before applying.
Download the application form from the official website: iiitm.ac.in.
Fill the application form carefully with correct information.
Attach self-attested copies of required documents such as photograph, signature, ID proof, and certificates.
Check all details carefully before submission.
Send the completed application form to the address mentioned in the notification through Speed Post / Registered Post or as instructed.
Ensure the application reaches before the last date: 01-06-2026.
Keep a photocopy of the submitted application form for future reference.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for ABV IIITM Non-Faculty Positions?
faq.answer: The last date to apply for the Non-Faculty Positions at ABV IIITM Gwalior is 01-06-2026. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well before this date through the specified offline mode.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available for Non-Faculty Positions at ABV IIITM?
faq.answer: There are a total of 2 vacancies for Non-Faculty Positions at ABV IIITM Gwalior. These vacancies are for the posts of Assistant Engineer (Electrical) and Junior Engineer (Civil).

faq.question: Q3. What is the minimum qualification required for Assistant Engineer (Electrical)?
faq.answer: For the Assistant Engineer (Electrical) post, candidates need a 1st Class Diploma in Electrical Engineering with 08 years of experience or a 1st Class B.E./B.Tech. in Electrical Engineering with 06 years of experience.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for the Junior Engineer (Civil) post?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit for the Junior Engineer (Civil) post is 32 years. Age relaxation is applicable for SC candidates as per the Government of India norms.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for General category candidates?
faq.answer: The application fee for General category candidates is Rs. 1000. For SC/ST/PWD candidates, the fee is Rs. 500.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for these Non-Faculty Positions?
faq.answer: The selection process for the Non-Faculty Positions at ABV IIITM includes a Written Test, Interview, Trade Test, and Game Test.

faq.question: Q7. How should the application form be submitted?
faq.answer: The application form should be submitted offline through Speed Post/Registered Post to the address mentioned in the official notification. The envelope should be superscribed with the name of the applied post.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for the Assistant Engineer (Electrical) post?
faq.answer: The Assistant Engineer (Electrical) post is offered at Pay Level 7 as per the 7th Central Pay Commission (CPC).

faq.question: Q9. Does ABV IIITM offer any age relaxation for reserved categories?
faq.answer: Yes, ABV IIITM offers age relaxation for reserved categories as per the Government of India norms. Specific relaxations are mentioned for OBC and SC candidates.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and application form?
faq.answer: The official notification and application form can be downloaded from the official website of ABV IIITM Gwalior. Links are provided in the "Important Links" section of this article.
